title: 6.6 year backtest on a basket of 8 crypto perps, 4,967 trades, out-of-sample Sharpe held above in-sample. Just went live, looking for holes before scaling.
author: Specialist-Depth-208
selftext:
Been iterating on a 1h bar systematic strategy across a basket of 8 of the more liquid crypto perps (BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, DOGE, LINK, AVAX). Pulled together what I think is a fairly defensible backtest:

Period: Sep 2019 to Apr 2026 (6.6 years, covers 2021 melt-up, COVID, LUNA, FTX, 22-23 chop, ETF rip, the 2025 mess)

Universe: 8 perps, equal opportunity to fire signals, no per-coin overrides or hand-tuning. Same params across all 8.

Sample: 4,967 trades distributed across the basket (not 4,967 BTC trades, which is the usual cherry-pick).

Cost model: 5 bps/side fees (Binance USD-M taker, VIP 0) plus real historical Binance funding rates spliced in hourly. No optimistic maker assumption, no zero funding hand wave. Conservative vs what real fills actually look like.

Returns:

* Total P&L: +$145,782 on $1k starting equity
* CAGR: \~112%/yr vs BTC HODL \~35%/yr (+77pp alpha)
* Holdout year (1.0y unseen): +119%/yr

Risk:

* Max DD: -21.6% (longest underwater stretch: 249 days)
* Sharpe 1.68, Sortino 2.14, Calmar 3.16
* Recovery factor: 410x ($145.8k gained per $31.4k DD)

Robustness:

* Positive months: 52 of 80 (65%)
* Profit factor: 1.33, Win rate: \~52%, Avg win 2.12% vs avg loss 1.64%
* Out of sample Sharpe retention: 112%. Train Sharpe 1.65 on first 5.6y, holdout Sharpe 1.86 on the last 1.0y the params never saw. Edge actually generalized.
* Basket-level signal, so no single coin carries the curve. Drop any one of the 8 and Sharpe stays in the 1.4 to 1.7 range (sanity-checked via leave-one-out).

The OOS retention is the one that matters to me. Backtests are easy, "still working on the year you didn't have when you finalized the params" is the only number I trust, and it held.

Just got it running live this week (proper bar-close WS, daily kill switch, hard TP, regime gating). PnL curve attached.

Not selling anything. No discord, no signals service, no course, no DMs please. Genuinely just want skeptical eyes before I scale. Happy to talk infra, walk-forward methodology, cost modeling, or which "obvious" ideas I had to throw out to get here. I also have a detailed log of every single trade over the 6.6 years.

If the curve looks too clean to be true, tell me where the tell is. That's what I'm here for.

[log pnl curve along with monthly returns for all years.](https://preview.redd.it/aiabfwdr59xg1.png?width=1002&format=png&auto=webp&s=2e134b9886f50b6f873ff7245da4f500ca5a8308)

comments.author: Opening-Berry-6041
comments.body: wow that out-of-sample Sharpe retention is seriously impressive like how did you even manage to keep that edge so consistent across different market regimes?

comments.author: d_e_g_m
comments.body: What's the strategy? Describe what it does, how it does it, when it does it
